entry.title: Clarification on Row Type "NORMAL" behavior
entry.content:
I am currently studying SAP Data Services (BODS) and have a question regarding how the Operation Code (Row Type) changes throughout a Dataflow. Specifically, I am confused about the different behaviors of the "NORMAL" flag depending on whether a Table_Comparison (TC) transform is used.

Based on the official documentation, I understand that:

  1. All rows extracted from a source are initially flagged as NORMAL.

  2. When a row flagged as NORMAL is loaded into a target, it is treated as a new row and an INSERT operation is performed.

I have designed a test Dataflow as shown in the attached image to compare two scenarios where the source and target data are identical (no changes).

 

Scenario 1: Source -> Map_Operation (MO) -> Target (DW_DATA_COMP2)

  • MO Setting: Normal to Normal.

  • Result: It triggers a Unique Constraint Violation error because the NORMAL flag from the source is treated as an INSERT. I understand this part as it aligns with the documentation.

Scenario 2: Source -> Table_Comparison (TC) -> Map_Operation (MO) -> Target (DW_DATA_COMP)

  • TC Setting: Comparing Source and Target.

  • MO Setting: Normal to Normal.

  • Result: The rows are Discarded (no SQL is executed) by the target.

My Questions: In Scenario 2, how is each row flagged at each stage of the flow when the source and target data match exactly?

  • Source Table Stage: Is the row flagged as NORMAL?

  • Table_Comparison Stage: Does the TC transform maintain the NORMAL flag because it's a "Match with no difference"?

  • Map_Operation Stage: If the MO receives a NORMAL flag and outputs it as NORMAL, why does the Target treat this differently compared to Scenario 1?

Does the Table_Comparison transform add some internal metadata or "instruction" to the NORMAL flag that tells the Target to discard it instead of inserting it? I would appreciate a clear explanation of how the engine distinguishes between these two "Normal" states.

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Inquiry Regarding Custom Tax Engine Integration and TDT Support in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private
entry.content:
1. Background Our client is a global enterprise operating in over 100 countries. We have developed a proprietary in-house Tax Engine.

  • B2C Business: Tax calculation is handled at the front-end sales system by calling the Tax Engine directly.

  • B2B Business: We plan to trigger the Tax Engine within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition to complete tax calculations for Sales Orders (SO).

2. Key Concerns & Limitations The client is concerned that the standard SAP tax configuration may not support their complex global requirements due to:

  • a. Tax Code Constraints: Standard tax codes (2 characters) have a 1-to-1 mapping with tax types/rates. Managing validity periods at the tax code level is restrictive (validation issues), making it difficult to handle multi-tax/multi-rate scenarios across 100+ countries.

  • b. Granularity: Difficulty in accommodating highly granular tax management and business classification requirements.

3. Evaluated Integration Approaches

  • Approach A (External Suite Model): Similar to Thomson Reuters/ONESOURCE. Using custom programs to sync MM/SD/FI document data to the Tax Engine and returning results to SAP via dynamic condition records.

  • Approach B (India GST Model): Leveraging a framework similar to the 2017 India GST solution. We need SAP to detail this architecture and any potential business/product limitations.

4. Specific Questions on TDT (Time-Dependent Taxes) We understand that TDT is primarily available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • Does the Private Edition fully support TDT for all 100+ target countries?

  • Can TDT resolve the aforementioned limitations regarding tax code exhaustion and validity date validation in a global rollout?

5. Request for Guidance

  • Could SAP provide architectural best practices for integrating a 3rd-party tax engine in a Private Edition environment?

  • Which approach (A, B, or a modern API-based service like SAP BTP Tax Service) is recommended for a global enterprise of this scale?

entry.title: HotNews3685270影響確認
entry.content:
お世話になっております。

■経緯
System recommendation情報にて件名HotNewsを確認

Solution Manager ST 720 0009

■確認依頼
①上記SolManシステムは、本HotNewsの対象であり、回避策はない（ノート適用もしくはSP適用が必須である）認識に間違いないでしょうか。

entry.title: Re: HotNews3685270影響確認
entry.content:
ご担当者様

平素よりお世話になっております。

お問い合わせにつき、下記回答させていただきます。

■確認依頼
①上記SolManシステムは、本HotNewsの対象であり、回避策はない（ノート適用もしくはSP適用が必須である）認識に間違いないでしょうか。

＝＞はい、ご認識の通りです。

②SolMan以外に本HotNews対象のシステムはない認識で間違いないでしょうか。

＝＞はい、ご認識の通りです。

③対応としてノート適用を実施する場合、3668705の後に3685270も必要（どちらかだけでは足りない）で認識あっているでしょうか。

＝＞あっていまま。ノート3668705と3685270両方ともご適用ください。

④対応としてノート適用を実施する場合の影響についてご教授願います。

＝＞システム動作及びビジネス運営などに影響しません。どうぞご安心ください。

⑤ノート適用を実施する場合、再起動を含み、他に必要な対応はありますでしょうか。

＝＞再起動などはいらないです。

⑥本HotNewsについて、外部攻撃懸念はない認識で間違いないでしょうか。

＝＞はい、外部攻撃懸念はないです。

以上です。宜しくお願い致します。
SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: Returned Quantity Field Does Not Allow Decimal Values in Perform Maintenance Job App in FIORI
entry.content: If the field in the CDS view or the OData entity is mapped to a data element without decimals (like MENGE vs. a whole number type), the Fiori UI will automatically mask the input to integers only.

entry.title: How to find the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting (ID: F3172) ?
entry.content:
The app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting (ID: F3172) is not visible in the Fiori Launchpad search.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to find the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Report...
entry.content:
Firstly, the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting is integrated as an add-on feature within the app Run Statutory Reports. Consequently, it does not possess its own dedicated Fiori tile, which leads to its absence in the Fiori Launchpad search.

Secondly, to locate the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting within the app Run Statutory Reports, the following requirements must be met: 

  1. Since the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting is a licensed feature of DRC - Statutory Reporting, activating scope item 5XU is necessary.
  2. Activation of this feature for the relevant country is required through the configuration activity Activate Document and Reporting Compliance Features (ID: 105518).
  3. After activation, updating the reporting task is necessary in the app Run Statutory Reports.

entry.title: Standard algorithm 001 finding matches outside defined parameter sets
entry.content:
Hi,

We are having an issue where some bank transactions are being automatically allocated to the wrong customer account.

We had this issue a while ago and believed this was because interpretation algorithm 001 was searching for unspecific references on the customer accounts (e.g. the word "payment") so we limited the reference document number range under Define Parameter Sets as per below:

This seems to have somewhat reduced the problem but I'm told we are still "randomly" getting transactions incorrectly allocated to the wrong customer accounts by interpretation algorithm 001, e.g. in the below case:

From a quick glance I believe these transactions are being allocated based on the fact that the word "payment" appears in the bank statement memo and as a reference on the customer accounts.

-Is there anything that would cause the algorithm to ignore the range specified in the defined parameter sets and search for components of the bank statement memo outside of this range? We are uploading the bank statements using "Manage Incoming Payment Files" not "Upload Bank Statements". Is there something we haven't configured correctly?

-Is there a way to identify exactly which string the algorithm used to match the bank transaction to the wrong customer account? The log in Reprocess Bank Statement Items doesn't give enough detail.

Thanks

entry.title: 【SAPパートナー必見】Joule for Consultants(J4C)検討の完全ガイド
entry.content:
こちらのブログは、Finn Backerさんのこちらのブログを参考に作成したものです。

― 2026年より、Development Fundの活用も可能に ―

【2026年1月15日アップデート】
「SAP Development Fund（DF）を使って、J4C向けのAI Unitsを購入できるのか？」という質問を多くいただいています。結論から言うと、2026年より可能になりました。詳細は本文をご覧ください。

Joule for Consultants（J4C）が、すべてのパートナーに提供開始

Joule for Consultants（以下 J4C）は、SAPが昨年にローンチした Per User Per Month（PUPM）価格体系の一部として、すべてのSAPパートナーが購入・利用可能になりました。

すでにSAP社内のコンサルタントは長期間にわたってJ4Cを活用しており、
生産性向上・ナレッジアクセスの高速化・品質向上といった大きな効果が報告されています。（詳細はこちらの資料の7〜9ページをご参照ください）
日本語資料は、じゃぱぽ Business AIページからご確認ください。
(じゃぱぽへ初めてアクセスされる方は、こちらからご登録ください)

特に注目すべきは、
小規模パートナーでも大きな初期投資なしで始められる価格設計になっている点です。

パートナーからよく聞かれる5つの質問

Q1. SAP AI Unitsはどうやって購入しますか？

方法は主に2つあります。

① SAP Storeから購入（条件付き）

以下の条件を満たす場合のみ可能です：

  • 過去に 旧AI Unitを購入したことがない

  • 購入履歴が SKU 8019164 のみ

該当する場合は、SAP Storeで「Buy as a customer」を選択して購入できます。

② SAPのAccount Executive（AE）経由で購入

  • SAP AE に見積を依頼

  • Partner Business Manager（PBM）に相談してもOK

必要なAI Units数の目安

  • J4C：1ユーザーあたり 月35 AI Units

  • 例：1ユーザー × 12か月 = 420 AI Units

  • AI Unitsは100単位販売のため → 500 AI Units の購入が必要

Q2. Joule for ConsultantsにTDD（Test / Development / Demo）はありますか？

ありません。理由は明確です。

TDDは本来、

「パートナーが顧客向けSAPソリューションを検証・デモするための環境」

一方J4Cは、

パートナー自身が“本番業務”で使うツール

つまり、
パートナー自身がSAPの“顧客”として利用する位置づけになります。
そのため、J4Cは 本番（Productive）価格表のみで提供されています。

Q3. SAP Development Fund（DF）を使ってJ4Cを購入できますか？

はい。できます。

2026年1月より、SAP Development Fundを使って
J4C向けのSAP AI Units購入費用の一部をリインバース可能になりました。
ただし、SAP Development Fundが利用可能なパートナー限定となります。

⚠️ 重要な注意点

  • AI Units購入前に、必ず PBMへ事前相談・事前承認を行ってください

  • DF適用条件や手続きはパートナーごとに異なるため、詳細はPBMに確認してください

Q4. J4Cを使うために、SAPの業務システムを本番稼働させる必要はありますか？

不要です。

J4Cは スタンドアロンで動作します。

  • SAP BTPのサブアカウント上にインストール

  • コンサルタントは 専用URL から直接アクセス

  • SAPの他の本番／TDDシステムは不要

Q5. グローバルで1つのJ4Cを使えますか？それとも拠点ごとに必要？

グローバルで1インスタンス運用が可能です。J4Cは他システムと独立しているため、

  • グローバルBTP上で1インスタンス運用

  • 世界中のコンサルタントが同じURLにアクセス

ただし、

  • IAS（Identity Authentication Service）でのユーザー管理は必要です

J4Cの最適な利用対象者は？

主なターゲットは以下です：

  • 機能コンサルタント

  • 技術コンサルタント

  • SAP導入・運用支援を行うコンサルタント

活用例

  • ジュニアコンサルタント：
    → シニアに聞かずに即時に知識取得、学習スピード向上

  • シニアコンサルタント：
    → ピンポイントな情報検索、新領域対応、最新情報のキャッチアップ

J4Cを顧客に販売することはできますか？

はい、可能です。

  • パートナーは SAP AI Units（SKU 8019164） を顧客に販売可能

  • 顧客は SAP for Me からJ4Cのアクティベーションを実施

社内IT部門やパワーユーザーを抱える顧客にとっても、大きな価値があります。

まとめ：すべてのSAPパートナーが、今すぐ始められる

Joule for Consultantsは、

  • 小さく始められる

  • 月単位で柔軟に使える

  • DFも活用できる

  • グローバル展開も可能

すべてのSAPパートナーにとって、現実的かつ効果的な投資対象です。

導入にあたっては、以下を理解しておくことが重要です：

  • AI Unitsの購入方法

  • J4Cを稼働させるBTP環境の選定

  • SAP for Meでのアクティベーション

  • AI Unitsの消費モデル

  • SAP BTP上でのセットアップ方法

これらの詳細な手順については、こちらのガイド資料をご参照ください。

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition：高级外币评估 (Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation)
entry.content:
业务背景

在业务上，我们都了解到大部分企业或多或少都会发生一些外币交易，那么随着货币汇率的不断波动，为了在期末能够按照即期汇率出具更准确的本位币报表或者集团货币报表就需要执行外币评估。在以前的On-Premise系统时代，大家普遍使用的是经典外币评估系统功能去实现这一业务需求，但步入云ERP系统时代后，随着并行会计核算和多币种会计核算方案的推出，经典外币评估方案就显示出一些明显的瓶颈，于是SAP在近几年推出了高级评估解决方案。

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 系统在前几年推出的高级评估解决方案中包括了“过账长期资产和负债的贴现”，“高级外币评估”，“过账基于信用风险的减值”和“过账资产负债表重新分类”四部分功能，本文旨在系统介绍高级评估解决方案中的“高级外币评估”功能。与经典外币评估相比，高级外币评估带来以下显著变化和优势：

支持并行会计核算（UPA – Universal Parallel Accounting）

支持多币种会计核算，例如功能货币，公司代码货币和集团货币

在会计报表版本中应用语义标记（Semantic Tags）概念和功能

支持直接过账至原始科目，包括未清项管理科目和统御科目

 

系统配置

激活高级评估

Activate Advanced Valuation (SSCUI 103315)

所有SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition新配置（provisioning）系统都默认激活高级评估，一旦系统存在过账数据后不得再取消激活高级评估。

 

定义用于在总账流程中确定科目分配的规则

Define Rules for Determining Account Assignments in G/L Processes (SSCUI 104827)

该配置路径中SAP预设置的配置内容不能新增，更改和删除。但您可以创建和复制自定义的科目分配规则。

 

将科目分配规则分配到高级评估

Assign Account Assignment Rules to Advanced Valuations (SSCUI 104826)

 

定义高级外币估值的规则

Define Rules for Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation (SSCUI 105450)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition系统中默认交付了以下2个评估规则。您也可以在此配置路径中创建自定义外币评估规则，并应用符合您业务需求的评估方法。

SKDV – 关键日期外币估值：默认应用始终评估（ALWAYS_VALUATE）的评估方法。

STDV – 时间相关外币估值：默认应用德国会计现代化法案（DE_BILMOG_VALUATE）的评估方法。即对于剩余期限少于1年的所有当前未清项目采用ALWAYS_VALUATE方法；对于剩余期限大于1年的非当前项目采用STRICTLY_LOWEST_VALUE（严格最低价值原则）方法。

 

在这一配置页面可以设置执行高级外币评估的汇率类型和评估方法。

 

在这一配置页面可以设置执行高级外币评估的语义标记。您也可以新增自定义语义标记后将其添加为执行高级外币评估的前提条件。

 

在这一配置页面可以设置执行高级外币评估的汇兑损益过账科目。

 

将外币估值规则分配到会计准则

Assign Rules for Foreign Currency Valuation to Accounting Principles (SSCUI 105451)

 

分配财务报表版本到会计准则

Assign Financial Statement Versions to Accounting Principles (SSCUI 103215)

这一配置将财务报表版本分配至会计准则，后续系统会按照分配的财务报表版本执行高级外币评估。

 

管理全局层次结构

Manage Global Hierarchies (F2918)

这一配置将执行高级外币评估的语义标记分配至具体的科目，包括统驭科目和总账科目。

 

经典评估补充配置

如果您还在使用经典会计报表版本配置，且未能将会计报表版本迁移至全局层次结构，请补充参考以下配置清单来完成高级外币评估的系统设置：

定义会计报表版本 Define Financial Statement Versions (SSCUI 102669)

分配财务报表版本到会计准则 Assign Financial Statement Versions to Accounting Principles (SSCUI 103215)

将语义标记分配到财务报表版本 Assign Semantic Tags to Financial Statement Versions (SSCUI 102659)

 

总结

综上所述，高级外币评估重新设计了其整个配置模型，拥有完全不同的配置路径，其配置逻辑大致为：评估规则 > 语义标记 > 财务报表版本 > 会计准则 > 评估规则。更多配置信息请参考 SAP Help Portal 高级外币评估的配置要求检查清单 和 SAP KBA 3219200 - How to Post Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation。

 

功能演示

维护期末汇率

系统应用：货币汇率 (F3616)

 

执行高级外币评估

系统应用：计划总账作业 (F1927)

高级外币评估需要分账套和公司代码执行。如果公司代码数量较多，为减少人工操作，建议创建重复运行作业。动态日历年度/期间“选项为创建重复作业提供可能性。当创建重复运行作业时，通常需要设置“动态日历年度/期间“，例如“本月最后一天”等选项，您也可以根据业务需求创建自定义动态日期选项。自定义动态日期选项请参考Fiori 应用“管理日期函数 (F2595A)” 相关功能。

 

显示高级外币运行结果

系统应用：分析关账运行结果 (F3753)

测试运行

高级外币评估有其默认的分组规则，如下图所示，未清项管理科目按照“日记账分录编号/会计年度/行项目编号/币种”分组；非未清项管理科目，如果是现金科目，则按照“开户行/开户行账户/币种”分组，如果不是现金科目，则以“科目编号”作为分组的标准之一。

 

正式运行

 

查看日记账分录

系统应用：管理日记账分录 (F0717A)

高级外币评估支持过账至原科目，无论其是否为统驭科目。按照下图显示，以供应商统驭科目 (K)的原始过账为例，虽然由高级外币评估触发过账至原统驭科目，但其科目类型已由供应商统驭科目 (K) 转换为总账科目 (S) ，且该科目依然会带有供应商信息。

 

基于本文的测试用例，高级外币评估将从交易货币 (00) 至公司代码货币 (10) 的外币评估定义为直接评估。不同系统的具体情况取决于系统中的货币设置，相关配置路径是“定义分类账和公司代码的货币设置”(SSCUI 103379)。

 

高级外币评估引入重新计量评估概念。基于本文的测试用例高级外币评估将从公司代码货币 (10) 至集团货币 (30) 的外币评估定义为重新计量评估。不同系统的具体情况取决于系统中的货币设置，相关配置路径是“定义分类账和公司代码的货币设置”(SSCUI 103379)。

 

区别于之前的经典外币评估，高级外币评估新增支持功能货币的外币评估。高级外币评估使用凭证货币对功能货币进行评估。如果尚未定义功能货币，则对公司代码货币进行评估。

 

经典评估VS 高级评估

基于前文内容，我们已经知道高级外币评估在系统功能方面相较于以前大家所熟知的经典评估有着许多大的变化。首先是运行方式的彻底改变，高级外币评估采用作业运行的方式实现，这缓解了对程序在线运行性能的要求；其次是报表显示的显著优化，以前的经典评估只能查看事实运行的结果，而高级外币评估得益于作业运行方式的实现，可以保留其历史运行结果，以便后续查看，且基于Fiori技术开发的报表，其用户体验也有较大提升；最后是评估逻辑的巨大变化，高级外币评估只采用增量评估的方式过账，对行项目级别数据进行评估，引入重新计量评估，过账至原始科目，且支持功能货币评估等。接下来，我们来简单总结一下经典外币评估和高级外币评估的差异内容：

 

经典评估

高级评估

系统应用是执行外币评估 (FAGL_FCV)

系统应用是计划总账作业 (F1927)

选择画面参数复杂

选择画面参数简单

基于评估范围和会计准则执行过账

基于分类账和会计准则执行过账

手工选择需要评估的科目

通过语义标记选择需要评估的科目

支持冲销评估和增量评估

仅支持增量评估

在科目余额层面汇总评估

对行项目数据进行评估

除汇兑损益科目外，统一过账至调整科目

除汇兑损益科目外，统一过账至原科目

通过配置基于货币类型进行评估

引入重新计量评估，默认对并行货币进行评估

不支持功能货币评估

支持功能货币评估

自动带出固定格式的抬头文本

日记账分录无抬头文本

自动带出所有行项目的固定格式项目文本

仅原科目过账自动带出固定格式项目文本

可通过配置更改外币评估的日记账分录类型

只能通过专家配置更改外币评估的日记账分录类型

支持重估应付暂估 (GR/IR) 科目

不支持重估税科目和应付暂估 (GR/IR) 科目

支持税科目重估 (SAP KBA 1589419)

不支持税科目重估

 

更多高级评估的限制请参考 SAP KBA 3290794 -Restrictions for Advanced Valuation in Financial Accounting Jobs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition。

截止到2026年12月SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 系统中的经典评估相关功能依然可用，但还是建议客户尽快在新的财年开始之初执行系统功能切换。经典评估功能的具体弃用时间表请参考：3686385 - Deprecation Timeline for Classic Foreign Currency Valuation functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Introducing AI-Assisted Multi-Model Data Extraction in SAP Ariba Invoicing
entry.content:
Why Invoice Data Extraction Matters

Today’s invoices come in many formats and languages, and extracting accurate data quickly is critical to keeping accounts payable flowing. As SAP Ariba Invoice enhances its existing invoice capture and data extraction capabilities already in use, such as SAP Document AI, SAP is constantly focusing on innovation and improvement in content data extraction. With this release, SAP Ariba Invoicing has added three advanced large language models—Anthropic Claude Sonnet, Google Gemini Flash, and SAP OpenAI GPT (which are all connected via SAP Core AI)—into the content extraction service. This multi-model approach improves extraction accuracy, reduces manual intervention, and helps accelerate end-to-end invoice processing.

Business Impact at a Glance:

  • Higher extraction accuracy: Combining multiple state-of-the-art models improves the system’s ability to identify and extract key invoice fields (supplier, invoice number, totals, tax information, line items, etc.) across a wider variety of invoice layouts and languages.
  • Fewer exceptions and manual corrections: Better initial data capture reduces the volume of invoices that require human review, lowering processing time and overall cost per invoice.
  • Faster throughput: Improved extraction quality means invoices move through validation and approval workflows quicker, helping you shorten your days payable outstanding and improve supplier relationships.
  • Scalable and flexible: The multi-model service is designed to handle high volumes and diverse document information, making it suitable for enterprises with complex supplier networks.

How the AI-Assisted Service improves Invoice Data Capture:

  • Intelligent model integration: By leveraging multiple models, the Invoice Data Capture service can better handle varied data elements and edge cases that a single model might struggle with.
  • Confidence-driven processing: Extraction results include confidence indicators, helping the system route low-confidence invoices to human reviewers while auto-processing high-confidence extractions.
  • Continuous improvement: Feedback from corrections and validations helps refine extraction performance over time, so accuracy improves as the system learns from real-world usage.

Security, Compliance, and Data Governance:

SAP Ariba Invoicing continues to keep enterprise data protection top of mind. The AI-Assisted Extraction is integrated into the SAP Ariba Invoicing environment under existing data governance and controls. For customers with specific regulatory, residency, or contractual requirements, SAP can provide details on how the feature aligns with your compliance needs and configuration options.

Getting Started with AI-Assisted Invoice Data Extraction:

The feature is available as part of the invoice capture updates in the latest release of SAP Ariba Invoicing. To enable or configure AI-Assisted Multi-Model Extraction, check your product release notes and administration guide or contact your SAP representative or implementation partner for assistance. Your team can pilot the enhanced capture feature on a subset of invoice streams to measure improvements before wider rollout by first enabling this feature in their test environments. Then when satisfied at the results, can be enabled in their production environment.

Final Perspective:

Bringing Anthropic Claude Sonnet, Google Gemini Flash, and SAP OpenAI GPT (which are all connected via SAP Core AI) into SAP Ariba Invoicing’s capture pipeline represents a significant step forward in applying the latest AI models to real-world finance operations. The result is a more accurate, efficient, and reliable invoice processing experience that helps finance teams spend less time on exceptions and more time on strategic work.

entry.title: Secure Parameter in AEM( Advanced Event Mesh)
entry.content:
Introduction:

A Secure Parameter is an encrypted configuration value used to store sensitive data (passwords, API keys, tokens and Client secrets). so, they are not stored or displayed in plain text. Advanced Event Mesh powered by Solace Pubsub+, hence secure parameter follows the same model.

 

 


Here in AEM (Advanced Event Mesh) Secure Parameter is a field used as password alias in the Advanced Event Mesh adapter configuration which only works while selecting the authentication type as Basic. 

 

How Secure Parameter Works:

  • Define the secure parameter once.
  • Advance Event Mesh encrypts it at rest.
  • Reference it by name in the configuration.
  • The actual value is never displayed again.

This can be achieved through below Example.

Navigate to the Monitor section in Integration Suite and click on the Security Material tile 

 

Click on the Create and select Secure Parameter option

 

After selecting the Secure Parameter, you will find the below fields 

Generally, we use name as Username in most of the times for our understanding, we can give any name.


Here Secure Parameter is nothing but the password we get after configuring the Advanced Event Mesh Account, you can use another blog for how to setup the Advanced Event Mesh there you can find the password which is used to connect to the Advanced Event Mesh.

 

 

After filling the password and click Deploy the changes will now be available in the Security Material.

We will now use this secure parameter in Advanced Event Mesh. The connection Parameters are same in both Sender and Receiver Adapter.

We need to the select the authentication type as Basic for using the Secure Parameter

 

 

Conclusion:
In Summary, Secure Parameters provide a Robust and secure way to handle sensitive credentials such as passwords and secrets without exposing them in Plain text. This blog walked through the end-to-end process from creating a Secure Parameter in the Integration Suite to using it in the Advanced Event Mesh with Basic Authentication highlighting how this approach simplifies credential management while align with Solace Pubsub+ security Standards. Overall, Secure Parameters help enforce best practices for secure configuration and reduce the risk of accidental credential exposure.

Happy Learning!  See you in next the blog.

entry.title: Get Certified in SAP Business Transformation Management topics
entry.content:
SAP Certifications provide a niche advantage to prove expertise in the related SAP topic. Business Transformation topics mainly focus across the topics on SAP Integrated Tool chain which help organizations to easily transform and digitize their organizations.

As most of you are aware there is a new certification methodology implemented at SAP which uses AI roleplays to test the knowledge of the candidates. This is an important milestone in the SAP Certification Journey. Read more about this new method of certification in this blog here Reimagining Certification.

Below are the list of BTM area certifications across various SAP Products – Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (CALM), Signavio, LeanIX and WalkMe. This list should help you in easily navigating the various certifications that you can achieve for this area. Alongside each certification there is also mention of which learning journey (with a link to the link journey) leads to this certification.

All the certifications in the BTM area are Scenario Based. The exam happens either using AI roleplay or Video recording.

 

Product Focus

Certification (Code)

Learning Journey

Integrated Tool Chain Products

SAP Certified - Positioning SAP Business Transformation Management Solutions (C_BCBTM)

Positioning SAP Business Transformation Management Solutions

Integrated Tool Chain Products

SAP Certified Associate - Business Transformation Consultant (C_SIGBT)

Exploring Business and Solution Transformation Tools and Methodology

Signavio

SAP Certified Associate - Validating Business Transformation (C_SIGVT)

Validating Business Transformations Leveraging Integrated Transformation Tools & Solutions

Signavio

SAP Certified – Process Data Analyst – SAP Signavio (C_SIGDA)

Analyzing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions

Signavio

SAP Certified – Process Management Consultant – SAP Signavio (C_SIGPM)

Managing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions

LeanIX

Learning to Use LeanIX for Successful Enterprise Architecture

WalkMe

Starting your Digital Adoption Journey with WalkMe

CALM

SAP Certified - SAP Certified Associate - Solution Transformation Consultant with SAP Cloud ALM (C_STC)

Transforming for Success with SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Re: #CoE#CSRI Can we migrate the configuration from one i...
entry.content: Hi Eric Please check following Technical Limitations, 1.Direct Configuration Transfer: Not possible to directly export configurations from one landscape and import to another independent landscape 2.Workspace Independence: CBC workspaces are independent - no transport path between different landscape workspaces 3.System-specific Restrictions: Each landscape maintains separate configuration environments and transport management systems

entry.title: Profit Center in SAP S/4HANA Service
entry.content:
Profit Center is a critical attribute of Finance and Controlling for Service processes as it directly affects cost and revenue posting which ultimately impact financial reporting and profitability analysis.

In SAP S/4HANA Service, the profit center attribute is supported in following service transactions:

  • Service Orders
  • Service Contracts

This blog covers the following:

1. Significance of item-based accounting for Service and profit center

2. Locating the profit center field in the UI

3. Profit center determination

4. Profit center changeability

5. Profit center exclusion

6. Profit center in follow-up transactions

 

1. Significance of item-based accounting for Service and profit center

Item-based Accounting: Item-based accounting is a concept to enable assignment of costs and revenues in Financial Accounting and Controlling to account assignment objects (also known as controlling object or CO object) that are related to the originating service order items and service contract items.

If item-based accounting is active for your service transaction scenario, profit center is mandatory for an item.

However, if the item-based accounting is not active, then profit center has no relevance in the service transaction. This means neither profit center can be determined, nor it can be entered manually for an item.

You can enable item-based accounting in Customizing using the following path:

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Enable Item-Based Accounting for Service.

 

2. Locating the profit center field in the UI

  • Profit center is an item-level attribute, which plays a crucial role in financial accounting.
  • For a Service Manager, the field is visible as 'Profit Center' in the Items tab on the service transaction as shown below:

 2.1. Service Contracts:

It is also visible in the item overview page, in the financial attributes tab.

2.2. Service Orders:

It is also visible in the item page, in the financial attributes tab.

 

 3. Profit center determination

The system determines the profit center on service transactions (like service order, service contract) by checking the following sources in a sequence of decreasing priority:

1. Substitution rule

2. Manual entry

3. Employee responsible

4. Default Configuration in Customizing

This means that the system will give preference to the substitution rule followed by manual entry, employee responsible, and finally default configuration.

3.1. Substitution rule

To define a profit center substitution rule, go to the Fiori launchpad and in the App Finder, search 'Manage Substitution/Validation Rules'.

For more information on how to create a substitution rule, see Substitution/Validation for Service Documents.

 When creating a rule, you can select or unselect the Overwrite flag.

I. Overwrite flag is selected:
The profit center defined in the substitution rule will have the highest priority. This means that if this rule is set, then even a manually entered profit center will be overwritten by the profit center defined in the rule.

II. Overwrite flag is unselected:
If the overwrite flag is unselected in the substitution rule, a profit center entered manually by the user or through an API will have higher priority than the substitution rule.

3.2. Manual entry

When a service manager enters a profit center on the item via UI or API.

3.3. Employee responsible

The system determines the cost center of the employee responsible from the item and then the profit center is determined as defined in this cost center.

Get cost center from employee responsible

TXN: PA30

Then get profit center from cost center

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Profit Center Accounting -> Assignments of Account Assignment Objects to Profit Centers -> Assign Cost Centers

 3.4. Default configuration in Customizing

If none of the above scenarios are successful in determining the profit center, then the system uses the default profit center configured for the controlling area.

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Financial Accounting -> General Ledger Accounting -> Business Transactions -> Document Splitting -> Edit Constant for Non assigned Processes

 

 4. Profit center changeability

  • The profit center can be changed while the life cycle status of the item is in status 'Open' or 'In Process'.
  • Profit center is not allowed to change, once there is an actual cost posting against the service item.
  • If a service contract is assigned and this contract is enabled as a root item in financial hierarchy.  If you assign a service contract and if the Customizing flag 'Root Item in Financial Hierarchy' is set on the service contract item category. Such a service contract item is then considered as a leading object for revenue recognition and can receive the revenue recognition key that represents the cost-based percentage of completion method. When a service document item is assigned to such a contract, its profit center is non‑editable (display‑only). This protects consistency across the hierarchy under collective accounting.  Configuration path to check if 'Root Item in Financial Hierarchy' is enabled:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Service -> Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Define Item Categories

 

5. Profit Center Exclusions

There are certain items for which profit center can’t be entered directly and must instead be entered on a higher-level item.

5.1. Service Hierarchy

A service hierarchy is a group of service order or service quotation items (e.g., main repair with parts/labour subitems) where the accounting and controlling for the whole group is managed through a single controlling object.

The profit center must be entered only on the main item of the service hierarchy, and all subitems inherits the profit center from the main item.

The below screenshot shows an example of service hierarchy where item 10 is the main item and item 20 is the subitem. Here, item 20 has no profit center assignment.

5.2. Price Item

Price items can only be used as the subitems of service items or execution order items. When you add a price item to a service order or a service quotation, the price item inherits the account assignment object and service contract from its higher-level item. The profit center is inherited from the higher-level item.

The below screenshot shows an example where item 20 is the price item in which profit center can't be entered. The profit center for price item is inherited from the higher-level item.

 6. Profit center in follow-up transactions

The profit center is an attribute which is automatically passed from service transactions to follow-up transactions or documents as mentioned below. 

  • Billing Document Request (BDR): Once the service transaction is released for billing and the BDR is generated, then open the 'Manage Billing Document Requests' to see the profit center in the BDR.
     
  • Sales Order: Sales order is created as a follow-up transaction from service order. Open transaction VA03 to see the profit center information.

 

 

  • Debit Memo Request (DMR): In the DMR, go to the 'Item Overview' to see the profit center details.

  • Actual/Plan Posting: Go to ‘Sales Performance Plan/Actual’ app and find profit center details.

 

 

Summary

In conclusion, Profit Center is a key element for Finance and Controlling of service processes. Hence, correctly configuring and managing profit centers in SAP S/4HANA service processes ensures accurate cost and revenue posting.

entry.title: SAP BTP HTML5 App asking login again and again
entry.content:
Hello Community,
I have recently started working with RAP programming and successfully developed a Fiori application using CDS views, OData services, and annotation-based UI. The app was generated using the Fiori Generator in SAP Business Application Studio, and the preview runs without any issues.
I then deployed the application to Cloud Foundry in SAP BTP. The deployment completed successfully, and I can see the app listed under the HTML5 Applications section. However, when I try to access the app, I am repeatedly prompted with a browser login dialog. Even after providing my valid BTP credentials, the prompt continues to reappear, preventing me from accessing the application.
I have followed the same steps as outlined in the learning resources, but I am unsure where the configuration might be missing or incorrect. Could you please guide me on what might be causing this repeated login prompt and how to resolve it?
Thank you in advance for your support.


Trust Config:

XS-APP.JSON:

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/sap/(.*)$",
      "target": "/sap/$1",
      "destination": "abap-cloud-abap-cc2459batrial-dev",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },
    {
      "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/test-resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}
XS-SECURITY.JSON code:
{
  "xsappname": "travelapp",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "description": "Security profile of called application",
  "scopes": [],
  "role-templates": []
}
MTA.YAML:

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: travelapp
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
- name: travelapp-destination-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  requires:
  - name: travelapp-destination-service
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  - name: travelapp-repo-host
    parameters:
      service-key:
        name: travelapp-repo-host-key
  - name: travelapp-uaa
    parameters:
      service-key:
        name: travelapp-uaa-key
  parameters:
    content:
      instance:
        destinations:
        - Name: travelapp_html_repo_host
          ServiceInstanceName: travelapp-html5-service
          ServiceKeyName: travelapp-repo-host-key
          sap.cloud.service: travelapp
        - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
          Name: travelapp_uaa
          ServiceInstanceName: travelapp-xsuaa-service
          ServiceKeyName: travelapp-uaa-key
          sap.cloud.service: travelapp
        existing_destinations_policy: update
  build-parameters:
    no-source: true
- name: travelapp-app-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: .
  requires:
  - name: travelapp-repo-host
    parameters:
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: resources
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - travelapp.zip
      name: travelapp
      target-path: resources/
- name: travelapp
  type: html5
  path: .
  build-parameters:

    supported-platforms: []

entry.title: Re: Confirming quantities with a handheld device in SAP HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @AngelT

Yes, you can use a handheld scanner in public cloud but automatically increasing the quantity after each scan depends on which receiving process you use.

I have some questions to enable me provide the right answer:

  • Is your client’s intention to scan one barcode per box and want the system to count each box?
  • Is the requirement to scan each carton barcode and the quantity keeps increasing by 1?
  • Will your client be scanning material EAN or GTIN repeatedly to count pieces?

Regards

Chris

entry.title: Z* Custom CDS Views Not Visible in SAC Live Connection (Only Standard CDS Views Appear)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I’m currently experiencing an issue where custom CDS Views (Z*) are not visible in an SAC Live Connection, and I would appreciate your advice.

I connected SAC to an S/4HANA PCE system using a Live (Direct) connection by manually configuring the host, port, and client. For testing purposes, I am using a user with SAP_ALL authorization.

The connection itself works fine, and standard CDS Views are visible and accessible when creating a Live model in SAC. However, CDS Views that I created by copying standard views are not appearing as data sources in SAC.

I have applied the same annotations as the standard views, including:

  • @analytics.query: true

  • @odata.publish: true

I have also activated the OData service successfully.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue where custom CDS Views do not show up in SAC Live Connection while standard views do? Are there additional settings or checks I may have missed?

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help.

Connecting Type : BW because of Direct...

 

<SAC Live Modeling>

Standard CDS View is ok 

CDS view : C_SalesOrderItemQry

SQL VIEW Name :  "2CCSDSLSORDERITEMQ" 

 

Same As Z* CDS View... not listed...

RSRT is working in S/4 HANA

Annotation is fine

Even I created : Odata Service

Are you asking for the cloud system maintenance/outage/downtime notifications?

  • KBA 2896942 - How Super and Cloud Administrators manage Cloud Notification subscriptions for other users - SAP for Me

You may also refer to KBA 3481022 to check the other different types of notifications in SAP for Me also the subscription information.

Hope this helps.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: How to configure managed approuter to serve CAP endpo...
entry.content:
Your approuter is working correctly: it will direct users to the UI after logging in, and will allow the UI (who knows the exact endpoints inside the srv) to access the backend data, taking care of passing required authentication.

Your srv is also working correctly. In production, there is nothing served on "/" because there is no need to. It is not meant to be accessed directly, only via the UI. If you're looking for the list of available services like you see in dev/test mode, see https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/cds-server#toggle-generic-index-page

FYI, you are not using a managed approuter but a standalone approuter (module: capbase). The managed approuter is the one included in your BTP subaccount via Work Zone. In order to use that one, you can (optionally) remove the capbase module and just access the applications via Cockpit > Subaccount > HTML5 Apps or via Work Zone.

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for the Chemicals (Together for Sustainability)
entry.content:
Chemical value chains are naturally complex. Products are diverse; production routes vary, and materials move through multiple transformation steps across global supply networks. This complexity makes emissions difficult to trace, methodologies hard to align, and results challenging to compare in a meaningful way. 

One of the major obstacles is limited upstream transparency within the value chain. Without consistent supplier-specific data, companies are forced to rely on proxies and averages to determine Product Carbon Footprints (PCFs) which may weaken the credibility of calculations and make effective decarbonization harder to achieve. 

Agnostic carbon accounting standards provide an important baseline, but they were never designed to fully capture the realities of the chemical industry. Methodological choices, such as system boundaries, allocation rules, or data granularity, can significantly influence results, leading to divergent outcomes even when companies follow the same high-level standards. This lack of sector-specific alignment is why initiatives like Together for Sustainability (TfS) were created, to translate generic guidance into a consistent, chemical-industry-specific approach that improves transparency and comparability. 

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management’s new multi-impact capability further supports this sector-specific guidance, by strengthening its alignment with the TfS calculation methodology and reporting categories. Embedding these requirements directly into Corporate Scope 3.1 (Purchased Goods and Services) inventories and PCF workflows and data structures, supports more transparent and comparable PCFs across chemical value chains, helping companies operationalize the TfS guidance at scale and move toward insights driven by high-quality, supplier-specific data. 

What is Together for Sustainability about 

Together for Sustainability (TfS) was created in response to a shared challenge across the chemical sector: sustainability efforts were being slowed down by unaligned approaches to Scope 3 and Product Carbon Footprints. Despite the existence of GHG standards that define the rules for PCF calculation, differences in interpretation and application across companies have led to PCFs and Scope 3.1 figures that are influenced as much by methodological choices as by actual performance. This lack of consistency makes data difficult to compare, exchange, or use with confidence. 

The objective of the TfS Guideline is to address this gap by defining a consistent, sector-specific approach to carbon data collection and calculation for the Chemical Industry. At its core, the guideline establishes clear boundaries and principles for PCF and Scope 3.1 accounting. It provides a uniform process for data collection and emission calculation, and a robust, audit-ready framework that can be applied consistently across companies and suppliers. In doing so, it harmonizes how PCFs are calculated in the chemical sector, reducing variability driven by interpretation instead of real differences. 

A central focus of the guideline is the integration of supplier-specific PCF data into both corporate Scope 3.1 inventories and downstream PCFs. High-quality supplier data is a prerequisite for meaningful cradle-to-gate calculations, enabling chemical companies to link upstream raw materials to their own production processes with better accuracy. This is particularly important in a sector where purchased goods often represent the largest share of total emissions, and where companies have significant influence over sourcing choices. 

How SAP Sustainability Footprint Management supports Together for Sustainability  

To support industry-specific standards like TfS, that include a specific categorization of impacts, SAP's Sustainability Footprint Management introduces a powerful multi-impact category feature. This enables you to map calculated impact category results directly to relevant reporting categories, ensuring alignment with TfS requirements. 

 

 

For this use case, the integration and calculation setup follow the same procedure as described in our "Multi-Impact Categories in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management" blog post. The process utilizes your ERP data to automate activity data collection, map to emission factors for the relevant impact categories, and execute one centralized footprint calculation. To support the TfS use case, it is important to ensure that the relevant impact categories for reporting are selected for the calculation 

Footprint Inventory setup 

In this step, you'll define the boundaries and specific impact categories to be assessed in your environmental footprint calculation according to TfS requirements, in this case, it would be cradle-to-gate. Here, the following categories should be selected, in TfS datamodel Version 3.1 September 2025: 

SAP Environmental Impact Category 

TfS Mandatory/Optional 

Climate Change 

Mandatory 

Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management 

Optional 

Fossil GHG Emissions 

Mandatory in 2027 

Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions 

Optional 

Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions 

Mandatory in 2027 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Emissions 

Mandatory in 2027 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals 

Optional 

Technological GHG Removal by Direct Capture of Industrial emissions 

Required under Condition 

Product Biogenic CO2 uptake 

Required under Condition 

 

For details, see Manage Footprint Inventory Scopes. 

Emission Factors for Calculation 

Additionally, you also need to ensure the inclusion of impact factors for the relevant categories using the Manage Emission Factors app. Each impact category requires a specific emission/impact factor. When populating either the LCA or Simple Template, input a separate entry for each category, even if they relate to the same product, energy source, or business activity, as they are distinguished by distinct Impact Category IDs. 

For TfS, depending on which impact categories selected, you must include the following categories are included via data packages: 

  • Climate Change (CLCH) 
  • Fossil GHG Emissions (CLCF) 
  • Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management (CCFL) 
  • Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions (CLCN) 
  • Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions (CLCLU) 
  • Land Management Biogenic CO2 Emissions (CLCM) 
  • Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals (RELM) 
  • Technological GHG Removal by Direct Capture of Industrial Emissions (RECCS) 
  • Product Biogenic CO2 Uptake (REPCC) 

For detailed guidance on maintaining emission factors data, see Emission Factors Data. You'll find a table under Impact Categories, listing all categories, including their IDs, dimensions, and indicators. 

Model Footprint Parameters 

Aside from the impact categories being reported, other calculation parameters like Manage Periodic Data, Energy Flows, and Footprint Parameters remain unchanged but must follow TfS requirements to comply with the standard. 

TfS details specific requirements around allocation of co-products and waste that can be set up on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Calculation Parameters such as Allocation rules and factors can be defined in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management under Manage Calculation Variants. This is important in multi-output process as TfS follows a strict allocation hierarchy.   

Transport 

TfS requires upstream transport, internal transport within a company site, and transport of intermediate products between sites to be calculated. Outbound transport to clients is outside the boundary scope. Transportation emissions shall be calculated on a well-to-wheel basis with allocation based on mass or volume. Additionally, aircraft emissions require special consideration to the Radiative Forcing Index. 

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Manage Transport setting app, allows to setup both supplier and internal transportation; mass allocation is supported. When calculating for TfS, if mode of transport Air is selected, you must ensure that the Emission Factors applied correspondingly take RFI into account when relevant for the calculation. 

Footprint Calculations 

A core principle of TfS is that GHG emissions are calculated by multiplying activity data by the corresponding emission factors (expressed in CO2e per declared unit). 

This is reflected in the Calculate Footprints app which includes all relevant data as defined in the Footprint Inventory Scope, Calculation Variant, and Period. Before starting the calculation, it’s important to verify that the footprint inventory data is complete to ensure accurate results across all selected impact categories. The calculation engine will then process all assigned impact factors against the activity data.  

Analyze Footprints  

For analyzing the calculated footprints, the Footprint Overview app offers a high-level summary of your impact calculation results, while the Corporate Balance app provides a consolidated view of your organization’s environmental performance. The Purchased Products analytics app now allows display of calculated footprints by impact category using a filter option. 

While the initial dashboards emphasize the Climate Change impact category, clicking on them allows for further detailed analysis. See Using the Footprint Overview Application for more information. 

The Corporate Balance app provides an overarching summary of your organization’s environmental performance. For more on viewing organizational emissions, see Using the Corporate Balance Application. 

The Purchased Products analytics app now provides a detailed understanding of the environmental burden associated with specific procured products across all selected impact categories, offering a richer perspective beyond CO2e. For more insights, see Using the Purchased Products Application. 

Map Calculation Results to Reporting Standards 

The Impact Categories from SAP Sustainability Footprint Management are designed to be standard-agnostic, serving as building blocks for supporting multiple reporting standards simultaneously. This versatility, however, requires final results to be mapped to the appropriate reporting categories for the chosen standard. The following table illustrates this: 

ID 

SAP Impact Categories for Climate Change Group  

Together for Sustainability Reporting Categories 

CLCH 

Climate Change  

PCF excluding Biogenic CO2 uptake  

CLCF 

Fossil GHG Emissions 

Fossil GHG Emissions 

CCFL 

Fossil GHG Emissions from Land Management  

Land Management Fossil GHG emissions 

CLCB 

Biogenic CO2 Emissions from Combustion  

n/a 

CLCN 

Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions  

Biogenic Non-CO2 Emissions 

CLCLU 

Land Use Change Biogenic CO2 Emissions  

Land Use Change GHG Emissions  

CLCLM 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Emissions  

Land Management GHG Emissions 

RELM 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals 

Land Management Biogenic CO2 Removals 

RECCS 

Technological GHG Removal by Direct Capture of Industrial Emissions  

CCS Technological CO2 Capture 

REDCC 

Technological GHG Removal by Direct Air Capture 

n/a 

REPCC 

Product Biogenic CO2 Uptake  

Biogenic CO2 Uptake 

 

 

This new feature focuses on environmental impact categories stemming from Life Cycle Impact Assessments (LCAs). Therefore, currently, there is no dedicated category for the TfS GWP Aviation emissions (cradle-to-gate), as this is considered part of the Distribution and Storage lifecycle step. The environmental impacts from Aviation emissions can be calculated under the Transport Mode: Air and consequently be included in the PCF under this lifecycle stage.  

Product Carbon Footprint including Biogenic CO2 uptake is derived by combining all relevant impact categories in line with standard requirements at the point of outbound publication of the PCF.  

As part of our ongoing commitment to expanding our capabilities to support TfS, we plan to include Carbon Content attributes in 2026, further enhancing SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to deliver comprehensive well rounded sustainability insights. Stay tuned for these exciting updates!

entry.title: What's new: SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning – 2602
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore the latest updates to SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP), focusing on the key highlights and new features introduced in release 2602.

 6 new solution processes for Sample Planning Area I_SAPIBP2:

With the release of SAP Best Practices for SAP IBP 2511, we began offering new solution processes for the planning area, Integrated Business Planning (I_SAPIBP2). With the latest release, we have added six additional solution processes: four in the area of response and supply, and two in the integration space.

For supply planning, we now offer two solution processes: IBP Advanced Supply – Supply Review – TS-based Supply Optimizer (7LG), and IBP Advanced Supply – Supply Planning – OBP Optimizer (7LP). These processes use the optimizer to create a supply plan based on given capacities, supply chain constraints, and cost rules. For these solution processes, we provide a new sample data file for cost rates. In addition, for the time-series-based process, we have enhanced the Planner Workspace to display, for example, minimum and maximum production supply key figures.

Enhanced Planner Workspace for solution process IBP Advanced Supply – Supply Review – TS-based Supply Optimizer

 The other two solution processes in the area of supply are IBP Advanced Supply – Deployment Planning (7LU) and IBP Advanced Supply – Transportation Load Building (7LV). In the deployment planning process, a reliable short-term deployment plan is created by distributing available supply to meet demand. Using the deployment optimizer and a mixed push/pull supply strategy, goods are pushed from the production plant to the central distribution center, where they are stored until they are pulled by the local distribution center.

The test script for Transportation Load Building describes a solution process that groups either planned distribution receipts from the deployment process or purchased products from suppliers into transportation loads and selects the optimal equipment type for each load.

Planner Workspace for solution process IBP Advanced Supply – Transportation Load Building

For distribution processes, the new solution process IBP Advanced Supply – Order-Based Planning Inbound Integration for Distribution Planning (7LT) describes how to use real-time integration to transfer master data and transactional data for distribution processes from the SAP S/4HANA system to SAP IBP.

The new solution process, IBP Foundation – Inbound Integration with SAP S/4HANA using SAP Cloud Integration (7LX), demonstrates how to integrate sales order history data. This is important for enhancing your demand forecasting capabilities. For the integration from SAP S/4HANA as the source system to SAP IBP as the target system, SAP Cloud Integration (SAP CI) serves as the middleware to transfer the data. In this solution process, the integration flow "Integrate Sales Order History Data from Add-on to SAP IBP" is used to integrate the sales history data. Data Integration Flow: Integrate Sales Order History Data from Add-on to SAP IBP

 

Business scoping

For the planning area I_SAPIBP2, the new feature "business scoping" has been introduced. The concept of this feature is similar to the partial copy option in the SAPIBP1 planning area. During planning area creation, you can define which IBP applications should be included in scope. As shown in the screenshot, you have the option to select the full business scope or, for example, only the demand planning part. This enables you to create a leaner copy of the sample planning area, as only the minimum required objects of the chosen business scope—such as master data attributes or key figures—are copied to the new planning area.

 Selection screen for Business Scoping

 

Adding Joule and GenAI capabilities

Another enhancement is the addition of the Joule explanation capability for time-series optimization results. The solution process IBP for response and supply—supply review—TS-based supply optimizer (18N) has been enhanced to demonstrate this feature. You can now use Joule to ask questions about the results of the time-series optimizer run, such as “Please summarize the planning run KPIs” or “Were there any issues in the planning run?” 

Joule explanation capability for time-series optimization results.

 

 Here some more useful links:

  • SAP Signavio Process Navigator to download all assets of SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning
  • Never heard about SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning – check out this short video for more information
  • In case you want to learn more about SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain I recommend our blog series. The first blog is available here.
  • What's New blog about SAP Integrated Business Planning 2602 is available here.

Thank you for reading our release highlights blog. Stay tuned for more updates and enhancements in future releases.

entry.title: Re: How to retrieve app usage (apps executed per user) fo...
entry.content:
Good day @sandeep_shankarampet 

Thank you for your question.

The public cloud does not provide any standard in system Fiori app that gives you a long term report that counts executions over several months. What you can do depends on whether you need to aggregate or track users.

You may consider the following options:

  1. For per user app execution counts of a period, I suggest you use SAP Cloud ALM Real User Monitoring. This is a better fit for your requirement because it provides transparency about usage and it supports an analysis view with details on Application and User Name plus execution facts. See https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/real-user-monitoring.html for more details.
  2. For app starts by month, I suggest you use SAP for Me Most Used Apps. Note that this shows applications starts for up to 12 months and you can export. See https://support.sap.com/content/s4m/help/portfolio/my/products/details/purchased-product-usage-tab/mostuseds4hana.html for more information.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Where-used list of method with code line
entry.content:
The where used functionality in eclipse, (Ctrl+Shift+G) while on the method you want to get the where used for, is very handy but does not give a table. It is good for finding usages and taking immediate action on it.
This where used gives all the findings in a hierarchical way per object (or package if that is chosen), it does not show the line numbers but you can just double click the objects to navigate to the line where it is used and filter on object type in case you look for a specific object type. Much better than the classic where used in GUI in my opinion.

entry.title: Re: How to find the logic where the particular value is changed for dynamic variable in debugger.
entry.content:
Very interesting question.
As far as I know there is not really an easy way for this, if there is I would also like to know.

How I tend to do this (had a situation recently):

  •  Put a break where you have a view on the field
  • Go to the stack trace and look back step by step to follow everything that happens with this field
    In a sense look at the flow backwards
  • If required set breakpoints on earlier detected places with the stack trace and restart the flow

In my case this led to a finding whereby standard coding made a IF ELSE that impacted the field in question much deeper in the stack and that way I could see why it took ELSE over IF and found an answer and solution.
Of course this is not always possible, for those flows where it works with global objects it gets more difficult and then I basically set a breakpoint on every where-used finding of the object that looks like a potential interesting spot.

entry.title: Re: Build Custom Journal Entry Posting Service Based On I_JournalEntryTP By Creating Own RAP Object
entry.content:
Hi @TakeZhou ,

Thank you for this excellent demo. I have another use case that is currently driving me to despair. Mybe you can give me a hint.

I have created an action in my BO that allows me to retrieve data from a third-party system. I save the data using managed create. The user is now supposed to check the data. They can make a test posting using a button. Then they should make the correct posting using another button.

Since I am no longer in create phase, I am unable to call up the I_JournalEntryTP~Post directly. I have now tried this via application job. It dumps in the action. But it can't be right that I have to save a dummy value via modify in order to get into the save sequence, can it? 

What do you think?

entry.title: SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) 2602 - Available Now!
entry.content:
SAP Integrated Business Planning 2602 is now generally available, and the new year starts with great news: Harmonized scenario management is finally here!

Harmonized scenario management is a new capability that unifies and simplifies how you work with scenarios across orders and key figures in SAP IBP. With harmonized scenarios you manage the complete scenario lifecycle in the Planner Workspaces app: You can create, modify, and promote both order and key figure data within a single scenario framework.

Key improvements include the ability to run simulations on top of scenarios for orders, extended scenario lifetimes that cover both orders and key figures, and new promotion options, including promotion for orders.

Harmonized scenario management offers controlled shine-through behavior that now includes orders, giving you better control of how baseline changes flow through your planning environment.

Alongside the harmonized planning area, this enhancement takes integrated planning in SAP IBP to the next level, letting orders and forecasts work together seamlessly without requiring you to manage separate processes.

 

 

Now let’s explore some of the other highlights of the first SAP IBP release in 2026:

 

Supply

Cut Waste, Boost Accuracy: Shelf Life Planning with Characteristics-Based Planning Attributes

We now combine shelf life planning with characteristics-based planning (CBP) attributes to strengthen inventory management, improve planning accuracy, and ensure delivery of products within their usable life, reducing waste and boosting customer satisfaction. Effective shelf life management also supports regulatory compliance and minimizes financial losses from expired inventory.

The new capabilities let you define shelf life for products based on specific characteristics, take expiration dates of planning elements (such as stock) into account, and select the correct batch by evaluating both product characteristics and shelf life.

 

 

Automate End-to-End Distribution with Intelligent Deployment Conversion

Our new deployment conversion run algorithm automatically converts stock transfer requisitions (STRs) into deployment requisitions. Unlike order-based supply planning runs that create STRs, the deployment conversion run only uses a subset of supply types that is configured as available to deploy, such as available stock and scheduled inbound deliveries.

The powerful algorithm leverages your existing supply plan to assign available-to-deploy supplies across your network, making data-driven deployment decisions that align with your business priorities. A comprehensive set of configurable parameters allows you to customize the deployment logic to match your unique operational requirements.

The system converts STRs based on requirement date priority, processing them systematically from left to right to ensure the most urgent needs are addressed first. You can limit both demand and supply horizons for deployment conversion, allowing you to focus on specific timeframes.

The algorithm considers backward offset adjustments for both demand and supply elements that fall in the past, ensuring comprehensive coverage of your planning requirements. Fixed STRs are automatically included within the deployment conversion scope, providing complete visibility across your distribution network.

When partial available-to-deploy supply situations occur, the system intelligently splits STRs while respecting your specified rounding values.

 

 

Maintenance Repair and Operations (MRO)

Instant AI Analysis of MRO Inventory Runs 

The new AI-Assisted MRO Inventory Analysis delivers a concise summary and intelligent analysis of MRO inventory planning runs, accompanied by a table that highlights the inputs and settings considered. Planners save time by having the data and all nuances involved in inventory planning available in one action. ​

This capability improves the understanding of planning results, increases adoption of planning parameters, and boosts the overall effectiveness of the operator. The one-click AI analysis is available directly in both Planner Workspaces and in the SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel, and you can review operator settings and configuration on the same screen where planning occurs.

 

 

Planning UIs

Seamless Approval Workflows in Planner Workspaces

New seamless approval workflows let you sign off plans directly in the Planner Workspaces app while viewing the full context needed to make fast, informed decisions. By keeping approvals where the supply chain planning data lives, planners and decision makers save time and avoid switching between separate systems, consolidating tasks into a single, more efficient workspace.

This capability supports formal approval points in complex processes like Demand Planning, ensuring alignment, accountability, and clear management sign-off before moving to the next step. By leveraging SAP Build Process Automation, gated process steps can be embedded within SAP IBP, using out-of-the-box workflow templates to control S&OP approvals.

Users can approve or reject tasks directly in Planner Workspaces and associate planning workbooks, filters, and versions with the workflows used to approve the start or completion of a process step.

For example, during a demand review, a demand planning manager can formally sign off the demand plan while viewing the appropriate aggregated demand data in Planner Workspaces.

 

 

Precise and Flexible Filtering with Advanced Operators

The advanced filtering capabilities that were recently added to the SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel and the Planning Filters app are now also available in Planner Workspaces, giving you consistent, powerful filtering across your planning UIs.

Advanced operators like "starts with", "contains", and "greater than" enable you to quickly pinpoint exactly the data you need.

Take product searches, for example: When you filter for descriptions containing "1.5 Liters" any new products matching this criterion will automatically appear in your results - no manual filter updates required.

Additional filtering options will be added in upcoming releases.

 

Foundation

Smooth Collaboration with Real-Time Microsoft Teams Messaging

Planners can now send real-time messages to Microsoft Teams channels directly from SAP IBP when saving key figure data or scheduling application jobs, sharing reason codes, comments, and contextual details. By bridging planning actions in SAP IBP with collaboration in Microsoft Teams, this integration reduces communication delays, improves transparency across planning teams, and ensures critical updates instantly reach the right colleagues - including, for example, stakeholders of your S&OP process who aren’t necessarily regular SAP IBP users.

The integration delivers faster collaboration through immediate visibility of planning changes in Microsoft Teams channels, reduced manual effort by removing the need for separate notifications or emails, and stronger accountability by keeping everyone informed about key actions.

Messages can be triggered when saving key figure data in the SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel or the Planner Workspaces app, or when scheduling application jobs, and you can select the target Microsoft Teams channel directly within SAP IBP.

 

More Information

  • SAP IBP What’s New Webinars 2602
  • 2602 What’s New: Help Portal Documentation
  • What’s New in Best Practices
  • SAP IBP Community Page
  • SAP IBP Roadmap
  • SAP Customer Influence Portal for SAP IBP
  • Questions? Ask here
  • SAP IBP Learning Journey

entry.title: Re: Unable to send XML file as email from Manage Electron...
entry.content:
Good day @pallavisri 

Thank you for your question.

The error you provided in your screenshot hints at missing or incomplete configuration for sending the source document by email or the recipient email is missing. This issue is addressed in KBA 3251019.

Here is a quick check you can carry out:

  • In your app select the document line, check the Log Icon and see what the detailed message is. The message “could not be sent” is only the wrapper message. The log will tell you whether it is no recipient or you have not configured the email channel. It can also be, you have not defined SOA.
  • Seems your document relates to sales/bill to customer which the system uses in billing document. Open your Manage Business Partner app, check that you have maintained a valid email address for the customer.
  • Make sure that “send by email” is configured for your eDocument process.
    • Practically, you can check in Manage Electronic Documents app, select the eDocument and use the Customer action (this button is visible in your screenshot) to see whether an email recipient can be derived or maintained before you send. See KBA 3251019 for more details.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to restrict unused applications within a catalog
entry.content:
Good day @quocdt5 

Hi

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you can restrict access, but it depends on what you mean by “remove the application”.

The public cloud provides the option to fully remove access by removing the business catalogs that contain the app from the business role. Note that you cannot remove the app if it shares the same catalog with other apps you still need. In this case you will need to decide whether you want to:

  • Keep the catalog or redesign roles using a different catalog set.

SAP provides the possibility for you to separate display and manage apps in different catalogs or organisational and value restrictions that controls the authorisation objects behind the business catalog.

You can practically control this by:

  • Assigning “display” catalog to read only roles
  • Assigning “manage” catalogs to power user roles.

Here is a short guide for your tenant:

Go to your Administration space 

open Identity and Access Management 

Open Maintain Business Roles 

Search for the role, click edit 

Go to Business Catalogs and identify the catalog that contains the app you want to restrict/remove. Remove it from the role and save. 

Optionally, you may also consider removing the tile in your “Spaces and Pages” tab so users do not see it anymore. This is just a visual clean-up.

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Withholding tax base amount not populating automatica...
entry.content:
Good day @seunabati 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your description and the attached screenshots, there are a two points to be aware of in public cloud:

  1. When your Tax Calculation is set to automatic, it is normal that the WHT Base Amount and WHT Amount fields stay blank on the WHT tab. The reason is, the system calculates them in the background and you must check the posted accounting document to see the calculated base and amount. See KBA 3578497
  2. In your WHT type configuration screenshot, you enabled manual indicators. With those flags, the system tends to shift towards manual entry and can prevent the automatic determination you want.

To enable automatic WHT, in your config:

  1. Correct the Withholding Tax Type control flags in your CBC. Uncheck w/Tax Base Manual, uncheck Manual W/Tax Amount, keep Post WTax Amount as you required and confirm your base amount logic.
  2. In your Definition Withholding Tax Codes, confirm the 2% rate on your screenshot is maintained correctly. Also ensure the base and minimum checks are consistent with your scenario.
  3. In public cloud, you must mark the supplier as subject to WHT and assign the correct WHT type and code in BP tax data, otherwise the system may not apply WHT at invoice and or payment.

You can confirm your WHT was calculated even if the invoice shows blanks after posting the supplier invoice by opening the posted invoice, then navigate to the Journal Entry and check for WHT related lines or WHT info.

If you want WHT at payment, ensure WTax for Payments is set in the WHT type. If not, the system will not withhold at payment as you expect.

A quick guide in your tenant:

  • Open Manage Your solution,
    • search and open “Configure Withholding Tax Types”, “Defining Withholding Tax Codes” and Setting up Withholding Tax for Customer and Supplier Business Partners.
  • Adjust flag, verify code and verify your BP.

You can then retest your scenario by posting a new supplier invoice and check the journal entry. See Defining Withholding Tax Codes and Configure Withholding Tax Types for more details.

 To summarise:

  1. In automatic mode, blank WHT amount fields in the invoice can be normal. Just check your accounting document. See KBA 3447879 for more details.
  2. Your configuration shows you enabled manual WHT, for you to automate, switch them off and enable WTax for payments if you require withholding at payment level.  See Configure Withholding Tax Types and KBA 3578497 for more details.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Customer Self Service Maintenance via SAP for Me of Accounts Payable/Dunning and E-bill Contact
entry.content:
Following the successful rollout of Customer Self Service Maintenance for finance-related contacts earlier last year via SAP for Me, we are pleased to inform you of a further extension of this capability, which went live on December 18th 2025. We are delighted to announce the Go Live of our latest phase of updating the Accounts Payable/Dunning and E-bill Contact(s) within SAP for Me.   

 

From the “Home” page in SAP for Me, you need to go to the “Finance and Legal” page, “Overview” tab.  

You will only see, under the “Your Finance Contacts” card, the contacts for your company that currently hold one or more of the following roles: 

  1. Purchase Order Responsible – Maintenance (PO Resp. MT) 
  2. Purchase Order Responsible – Cloud (PO Resp. Cloud) 
  3. Maintenance Responsible (MT Resp.) the person who should receive the Consumer Price Index (CPI) notification 
  4. Accounts Payable/Dunning Contact 
  5. E-Bill Contact, including: 
  • E-Billing (all Lines of Business (LoB)) 
  • E-Billing Software and Maintenance 
  • E-Billing Cloud  

 

 

You might notice that there are three dots showing at the top right-hand side of the card.  

If you click on this, you will be able to see a more detailed explanation of “What the card is about?” and information of “Required Authorizations”.  

You can filter on each roles, applicable countries and status.  

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Add Role  

Click on the pencil-shaped icon as the edit icon located on the right-hand side of the contact you would like to update. The “Edit Contact” screen will open, where you can view the selected contact’s core information such as first name, last name, and job position, as well as the roles currently assigned. 

In the lower section of this screen, Accounts Payable/Dunning Contact and E-Bill Contact roles can be added by selecting the applicable country from the Applicable Countries options. (E-Billing (all LOB), E-Billing Software & Maintenance, E-Billing Cloud). 

After making the selection, click “Save.” There will be three blue dots that circle whilst the update is in progress. You will now be able to see the contact in the card with the new role assigned. This will also be assigned real time in our back-end systems.    

 

 

 

Create Contact  

If you want to create a contact, click on the “Create” button at the right-hand side of the “Your Finance Contacts”. This brings up a screen where you need to input the mandatory fields – First Name, Last Name, Email, and Job Position, then click “Create Contact”. Mandatory fields are marked with a red asterisk. 

 

A confirmation message will appear indicating that your request is being processed and may take a few minutes. You can continue working during this time. To check the latest status, refresh the card. 

If you enter an e-mail that already exists against a contact in our database, you will receive a warning message that you need to use the existing contact, by adding a role and choosing the e-mail address of the existing contact, rather than creating a duplicate contact.    

Transfer  

Click on the “Transfer” button to the right-hand side of the “Your Finance Contacts”.  

This brings up a screen where you can see the roles you want to transfer. By clicking the downward arrows on the right side, you can view the available options for different LOBs. 

After making your selection, you can search for the contact you want to transfer the role to at the bottom of the screen. Select the contact and then click “Submit”. 

If you would like to transfer a role to a new contact, you must first create the contact. Then, on the role transfer screen, search for the contact using the search field and select it to proceed with the transfer. 

Edit 

This is used for removing or adding a role. You can also edit a contact’s name or e-mail address as long as they are not S user in which case Super Admin will have to edit.    

Click on the pencil-shaped icon as the edit icon. On the screen that opens, you can make the required changes. Click the downward arrow to select the role you want to add or remove, then click “Save”. 

 

Note that you cannot remove a role, unless there is someone else in the company that has the same role.  

Delete  

This is used to delete a contact and break the relationship between the company and the contact.  This will not only remove the contact from “Your Finance Contacts” card but will also remove the contact from the back-end systems.   

There are two caveats to this. There must be someone else with the role assigned, and most importantly, the user must not be an S User.    

Click on the pencil-shaped icon as the edit icon and then “Delete Contact” button.  You will first be asked to ensure that there is another person with that role.  If not, you will need to assign that role to someone else first.  Once you have confirmed “Delete”.  If the user you want to delete is a S User, then you will only be able to remove the role, but the contact will not be deleted in the back-end systems. 

 

 

 

In short, customers can update their own "Purchase Order Responsible Maintenance, Purchase Order Responsible Cloud and Maintenance Responsible, the person who should receive the CPI notification. 

Following the go-live, Accounts Payable/Dunning and E-Bill contacts can also be maintained by customers via self-service. 

Should you have any questions, feel free to reach out to our customer service for assistance.     

Thank you for using “SAP for Me”.

entry.title: Re: WebSocket RFC – RFC For the Internet
entry.content:
Hi Gregor,

node-rfc is based on the NW RFC Library, so it should already automatically support WebSocket-RFC?! Instead of ASHOST and SYSNR just pass WSHOST and WSPORT in the logon parameter array, and that's it. If you want to do certificate-based logon, replace the user & password parameters with necessary TLS parameters (TLS_CLIENT_PSE etc.) see the documentation in sapnwrfc.ini.

(I hope, the developers of node-rfc were so smart to just pass any logon parameters to the NW RFC library unchanged, then every feature of the NW RFC library is automatically usable without modification on node.js level.)

Not sure about cds-rfc, though. I tried to google a bit, but didn't find the technical details about it. Is it also based on NW RFC library?!
