The SAP Note below outlines the roles and privileges required - extremely useful SAP Note

SAP Note 3463326 - SAP Datasphere: Metadata Import from SAP S/4HANA On-Premise via "CSN" https://me.sap.com/notes/3463326

The following SAP Note focuses more on the ESH_SERV´CH_SRV which you are going to have to setup for importing CSN entities.

SAP Note 3283282 - Implementation note: Infrastructure for metadata import of CSN entities

https://me.sap.com/notes/3283282

One quick tip when using PFCG to create custom roles,  create a single role like Z_DATASPHERE and copy the required Authorization Objects to it, with the privileges mentioned in SAP Note 3463326 - SAP Datasphere: Metadata Import from SAP S/4HANA On-Premise,

entry.title: Loss Handling in the Consolidation under Equity method
entry.content:
Dear Experts

We have encountered a scenario in consolidation where one of the entities is accounted for using the equity method, with a shareholding of 35%. Unfortunately, the entity is currently reporting losses, and these losses have exceeded the carrying amount of the investment.

As per IFRS, the investment cannot be written down to zero, and the company does not plan to infuse additional capital. In the subsequent year, once the entity starts generating revenue, these losses will be offset, and the investment will reflect its actual value.

We are currently unable to locate system settings to handle this scenario in consolidation. Could anyone provide guidance or the steps to correctly manage such a situation in the system while remaining IFRS-compliant?

Regards 

Srinivas Thatikonda

entry.title: Promotion Creation Reimagined: AI‑Assisted Single & Bulk Workflows in SAP Revenue Growth Management
entry.content:
1. Promotion Creation Friction: Lost Hours and Increased Errors 

For Key Account Managers (KAMs) in the fast-moving consumer goods industry, time is revenue. Yet significant portions of that time are consumed by meticulous, repetitive tasks. Creating a single promotion can be time-consuming — because KAMs need to look up past promotions, select dates and tactics, and manually enter information. That friction not only consumes time but also increases downstream errors in funding, claims, and reconciliation. What if KAMs could reclaim those hours and focus on strategic account growth instead? 

 

2. AI-Assisted Promotion Creation: Embedded in SAP Revenue Growth Management 

To address this challenge, SAP Revenue Growth Management is shipping two embedded AI features: SAP Revenue Growth Management AI‑assisted Trade Promotion Creation (hereinafter “AI-Assisted Single Promotion Creation”) and SAP Revenue Growth Management, AI‑assisted prompt‑based bulk trade promotion creation (hereinafter “AI-Assisted Bulk Promotion Creation”). 

Today, both AI features are embedded directly in the promotion creation workflow, where KAMs need them — empowering teams to work smarter and faster without further navigation. 

For more information on our current capabilities, see An Overview of SAP Revenue Growth Management. 

 

3. Three Ways AI Improves Promotion Creation: Precision, Scale, Control 

3.1. Single Promotion Creation: Name It and Let AI Fill the Rest   

When KAMs want to create a more precise promotion with less effort, they can use AI-Assisted Single Promotion Creation.  

Workflow at a glance

From an Account Plan, KAMs choose Create Promotion and enter a promotion name—for example, "limited Coffee, Christmas promotion, 10% off"—and the AI gets to work. AI offers inline, account‑aware suggestions for promotion details, dates for sell-in and store execution, product groups, trade spends and volume — all of which KAMs can accept or override.  

  • The promotion name serves as the primary cue in Single Promotion Creation because KAMs consistently encode dates, customer and tactic cues in naming conventions — and those cues are powerful, structured inputs for relevant, explainable AI suggestions. 

  • Behind the scenes, the embedded AI will explore the account assortment and product categories within the product hierarchy, identify and disambiguate the possible products inferred in the prompt. Trade Spend recommendations can recognize and utilize whichever trade spends are configured for the account plan. 

 

Significant Time Savings

AI pre-fills most promotion setup fields, including selecting a most appropriate promotion type, setting date ranges for promotion execution and sell-in as well as choosing the spend types, populating requested discount and lump-sum values and making uplift volume recommendations, based on the context of the promotion name and the selected account. Experts estimate this reduces average promotion setup time from around 15 minutes to roughly 3 minutes— a 75% efficiency gain.  

 

Adaptive Learning from User Interactions

The Smart Field Memory in this AI feature learns from KAMs’ manual adjustments. AI‑generated suggestions appear in a pale purple, so they’re easy to spot. When a KAM edits a suggested field and saves, the system logs that override and weight future suggestions for the same account and similar promotion names toward the adjusted value. Over time the AI becomes a personalized assistant—reducing corrections, improving consistency across promotions, and boosting KAM productivity. 

In short, AI-Assisted Single Promotion Creation elevates efficiency by enabling contextual field suggestions and adaptive learning, going beyond simple auto-fill through LLM‑driven contextual understanding of users' intent. 

 

3.2. Bulk Promotion Creation: Enter a Prompt, Generate, and Iterate at Scale 

While single promotion creation handles precise campaigns, KAMs also need to plan multiple promotions quickly to test strategies and ensure portfolio coverage, or to quickly generate a broader set of promotions to test different strategies across their portfolio. For this "speed-to-market" need, KAMs can turn to AI-assisted prompt-based bulk trade promotion creation in SAP Revenue Growth Management.  

Workflow at a glance

From an Account Plan, KAMs choose Create Promotions with AI and enter a natural-language prompt that specifies how many promotions to generate (1–12). KAMs can optionally guide the AI with past promotion data or upload attachments. The AI then generates draft promotions, including promotion details, product groups, trade spends, uplift volume, and provides explainable reasoning detailing how it has interpreted the prompt and what choices it has made in the suggested promotions. KAMs can accept promotions they like or regenerate as needed. When KAMs are mostly satisfied with the generated results, they choose "Save as Drafts and Edit" to refine promotions in full-edit mode before saving them as drafts or choose "Create as Drafts" to keep all generated promotions without further editing. 

 

KAMs can try different types of prompts – for example, KAMs could provide prompts like: 

  • Create 3 summer promotions for limited-edition coffee and coffee capsules with a 25% TPR on-invoice discount and a display for $5000, each promotion should be for 12 days, vary the products promoted in each promotion. 
  • Create a year-long EDLP agreement for dark coffee at 3% 
  • Create 4 promotions for 1 week each in Q4 for the chilled drinks category with an on-invoice discount of 20% and 30% volume uplift 

 

Four Ways to Enhance AI Results

To enter a prompt, KAMs can use natural language or switch to an even more reliable option — Guided Prompt. With Guided Prompt, KAMs fill in or choose values for controlled fields (e.g., number of promotions, promotion type, products, and spend type), and benefit from context engineering, making prompts more precise for tailored results. In addition, KAMs can ground the AI with past promotion data or by uploading attachments. These inputs together provide the AI with the best possible fuel — targeted data and prompts — so it can generate accurate, relevant promotions in seconds. When KAMs start working with a new account or lack historical data, the feature falls back to cold start heuristics business rules (e.g., holiday patterns, common uplift assumptions), so KAMs still get sensible defaults. 

 

Speed and Accuracy Balanced

Bulk Promotion Creation empowers KAMs to rapidly generate many plausible drafts and test tactics at scale without creating each promotion manually. We preserve that balance with a single review step that pairs rapid generation with clear reasoning and edit controls, enabling KAMs to validate suggestions quickly without compromising precision. After generation, KAMs can review all suggested promotions. To check the AI's logic, KAMs click "View Reasoning" to see the prompt interpreted, assumptions made, tasks completed, and any conflicts the AI identified and resolved — helping them accept useful suggestions faster and correct those that don’t fit. 

To sum up, AI-Assisted Bulk Promotion Creation offers a fast‑iteration mode with speed and accuracy, using practical levers to improve results and clear reasoning to support decision-making. 

 

3.3. Continuous Learning with Human-in-the-Loop 

The intelligence within SAP Revenue Growth Management is designed to be dynamic, not static. It evolves from a capable assistant into a trusted partner by learning from every interaction, ensuring suggestions become more accurate and better aligned with business over time, while users retain full authority over the final output. 

  

Self-Learning AI: From Manual Edits to Better Suggestions 

The table below summarizes the key AI enhancement levers available in the two AI features of SAP Revenue Growth Management to drive efficiency and accuracy. 

 

AI Enhancement Levers 

AI-Assisted Single Promotion Creation 

AI-Assisted Bulk Promotion Creation 

Smart Field Memory 

√ 

  

Guided Prompt 

  

√ 

Uploaded Attachments  

 

√ 

Past Promotion Data  

√ 

√ 

Heuristics Business Rules for Cold Start 

√ 

√ 

This table highlights a key synergy: data from past promotions in Bulk Promotion Creation enhances the AI's contextual understanding and can improve suggestions in Single Promotion Creation, forming a cohesive and self-improving system. 

Importantly, these capabilities also reduce downstream errors: consistent, account‑aware suggestions and grounded inputs cut the manual‑entry mistakes that cause funding, claims, and reconciliation headaches. 

  

Human-in-the-Loop Control 

A foundational design principle is that the AI provides suggestions, not commands.  

  • The experience is built around low-risk, high-efficiency interaction. All AI-generated content is clearly identifiable, fully editable, and predictable. KAMs can easily accept, edit, or discard any suggestion, ensuring they're rarely stuck with incorrect pre-filled text that adds manual work.  

  • Each step in promotion creation requires KAMs' explicit confirmation to proceed. This provides a crucial checkpoint, reinforcing trust and ensuring KAMs have the final say on every detail before any promotion is saved. 

In conclusion, this combination of continuous learning, explainable reasoning and human control builds KAMs' confidence in the AI and ensures the AI features truly augments human expertise to drive productivity and accuracy. 

 

4. Co-Innovation on AI-Driven Promotion Excellence 

These AI features are rolling into SAP Revenue Growth Management. They aim to reduce routine work and unlock new levels of efficiency, enabling KAMs to focus on customer strategy rather than form‑filling.  

Whether you're a forward-thinking KAM or a revenue growth leader, if your team is looking to:  

  • explore AI-Assisted promotion creation integrated with Joule 
  • test bulk promotion generation with customers' historical data or attachments 
  • test AI-Assisted deal sheets using a default template or a third-party template 

Get in touch at RGM_Engage@sap.com. Let’s explore a pilot that matches your account workflows and helps solve your most pressing challenges. 

Final Note:  

These new AI features in SAP Revenue Growth Management are being prepared for release and will be generally available soon.

entry.title: Der erste Stammtisch Basel - Freiburg
entry.content:
Endlich war es soweit, der erste Stammtisch hat in Basel stattgefunden. In gemütlicher Runde haben wir uns über SAP,  ABAP, Fiori und KI ausgetauscht. Uns kennengelernt und beschlossen, dass auf jeden Fall zu wiederholen 🙂

Der nächste Stammtisch wird in Freiburg stattfinden. Einfach diese Gruppe abonnieren und das Event mitbekommen! Details folgen dann. 

Bastian
